lines.text: This is your Operations Overview, the live picture across the corridor at a glance.
It's the first thing you open at the start of a shift, because it answers the only questions that really matter.
How much money are we holding, how much have we earned, and how many jobs are in motion.
The first figure, Locked Escrow, is the total client money Nguzo is currently holding in trust.
This is the heart of the model, that money is real and tracked, but not released until work is signed off.
Platform Revenue is the seven percent service fee Nguzo earns once jobs settle.
That single fee is how Nguzo makes its money, fairly and transparently, the same percentage on every deal.
Then your counts, active contracts on the network, and the suppliers registered to carry them.
Assets shows how much verified machinery and how many trucks are available across your suppliers.
And Breakdowns, which turns amber the moment a machine reports a fault in the field.
When that counter lights up, you know a job needs attention before it stalls.
Below the counters sits every contract in the system, with its route, escrow, fee, and status.
Each row tells you whether it's a domestic move or a cross-border one, and where it stands in its life cycle.
In one screen you can see exactly how much money is in trust and where every job stands.
